nodes.name: 南非
nodes.summary: 南非是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联45种鸟类，包括草黃樹鴨、非洲黑鴨、Shelley's francolin等45种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 肯尼亚
nodes.summary: 肯尼亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联51种鸟类，包括索马里鸵鸟、南非鸭、Western crested guinea fowl等51种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联1443种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等1443种鸟类。

nodes.name: 塔納河
nodes.summary: 塔納河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括黃嘴䴉鸛，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 索马里
nodes.summary: 索马里是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联24种鸟类，包括索马里鸵鸟、Eastern crested guinea fowl、Yellow-necked spurfowl等24种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 非法猎捕
nodes.summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

nodes.name: 鹳形目
nodes.summary: 鹳形目（Ciconiiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.name: 黃嘴䴉鸛
nodes.summary: 黃嘴䴉鸛（Yellow-billed stork，学名 Mycteria ibis），分类上属于鹳形目，并归入鹳科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联9个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括非法猎捕。

nodes.name: 马达加斯加
nodes.summary: 马达加斯加是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联21种鸟类，包括草黃樹鴨、厚嘴棉鳧、馬島潛鴨等21种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is widespread south of the Sahara and also occurs in Madagascar

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is widespread south of the Sahara and also occurs in Madagascar

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|Flock in a seasonal wetland, Zambia The yellow-billed stork occurs primarily in Eastern Africa, but is widely distributed in areas extending from Senegal and Somalia down to South Africa and in some regions of western Madagascar. During one observation of a mixed species bird colony on the Tana River in Kenya, it was found to be the commonest species there, with 2000 individuals being counted at once. It is also the dominant species in large breeding colonies at Lake Manyara and the Ihefu wetlands fringing the Ripera River in Tanzania. The species does not generally migrate far, at least not outside its breeding range; but does usually make short migratory movements which are influenced by rainfall

During one observation of a mixed species bird colony on the Tana River in Kenya, it was found to be the commonest species there, with 2000 individuals being counted at once. links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: As well as being abundant and widespread, the yellow-billed stork also appears tolerant of short-term natural habitat changes. However, in East Africa, it is known to be at risk from poaching and habitat reduction despite abundance and population stability and is listed under the African-Eurasian Waterbird Agreement (AEWA). Nevertheless, the overall population is not currently considered to be threatened with serious decline, especially since breeding success is relatively high. In East Africa where it is most abundant, broods of 1-3 per nest have been recorded. Alongside human activities, natural enemies include cheetahs, leopards and lions, which all sometimes prey on this species. Eggs may be also at risk of predation by African fish eagles. At one colony in Kisumu, Kenya, approximately 61% of eggs counted between all nests hatched and 38% were predated by fish eagles. The success rate of fledglings was only 0.33 young per nest. However, increased egg predation by fish eagles has been reported to be linked to decline in fish stocks in the Winam Gulf
